rules.reason: Recursive force delete with wildcard or absolute/home path. Verify the target before proceeding.
rm -rf / detected. Refusing without explicit human confirmation.
Force push rewrites remote history. --force-with-lease is safer; verify no collaborator commits will be lost.
git reset --hard discards uncommitted work. Confirm there is nothing worth keeping in the working tree.
Destructive schema operation. Confirm backup and target environment before proceeding.

Production-mutating command detected (apply/edit/delete/destroy/etc. targeting prod). Confirm change is reviewed, tested, and intentional. Read-only inspection commands (get/list/describe/logs/...) are not gated.
--no-verify skips pre-commit hooks. Don't bypass the safety net unless explicitly authorized.
Possible credential in command. It is redacted on the way into the chronicle (v0.2), but confirm it isn't being written elsewhere in cleartext.
Grok-specific high-risk direct mutation. Grok is equal peer but still passes through the same WITNESS floor as Claude.
